author: @billkelly4704
text:
Toy Story 5 is more of a sure thing just because it is a kid's movie. There are usually at least 2 huge kids movies, and this year those are Toy Story 5 and the Mario Galaxy movie. I don't think the Lightyear movie will have an effect, most know it really had nothing to do with the Toy Story movies. As for The Odyssey, just because it comes from Nolan does NOT guarantee a billion. Oppenheimer was contemporary, it was about something most are interested in, and it did have the whole "Barbenheimer" thing helping it. The Odyssey is different. It has the potential, but a movie about a time thousands of years ago isn't a sure thing, and Nolan has had some flops over the years. It will really come down to word of mouth, it'll need to have legs because it isn't going to break any opening weekend records, it's just not that kind of film.

As for Doomsday, I think it will struggle to go much beyond a billion, assuming it hits a billion. There is huge mistrust of Marvel now, and people will need to know it is different from the previous several films. Spiderman is more of a guaranteed hit, and if it does somehow lead into Doomsday, even with just a postcredits scene, that will help Doomsday a lot. Word of mouth will ultimately decide if it gets to a billion and beyond. If it sucks, it will fall off a cliff after a week or so.

author: @Aristocles22
text: The Odyssey has racewashing in it. It's one of the most important myths ever written, absolutely foundational to the West, and they spat in it. It's probably one of the last scalps woke will ever claim, seeing as wokeness is on its way out, but this is one of the last big blows it will inflict on us, at least until some version of woke returns in the distant future.

author: @jonmurphy4889
text:
The Russo Brothers like to repeat... They always have a big shocker in the opening of their Marvel films.

The big shocker in Doomsday will be Steve Rogers death at the very beginning of the film... This is why he's not referred to as Captain America and it says Steve Rodgers will return. 

Just like the Hulk and Thor getting their asses kicked in Infinity War as well as Heimdall and Loki getting killed to show how much of a threat Thanos was.

There is no buildup to Doom so they need a big shocker to show how much of a threat he is. 

Steve will see Tony's face and it will feel like the ultimate betrayal. 

I mean think about it, this character literally has no buildup nothing like what they did with Thanos or even Kang at this point... 

Also once again people think they want it but they really don't just like most of Phase 4 and 5.

All of these characters were hyped up and they all ended up being nothing or forgotten and they literally have to bring back old legacy characters in different roles...

It's a joke, a bad joke.

author: @BobSurce
text:
Meh. 🤷‍♂️

Odyssey?  I love Nolan's movies, too.  But just because I also love 'The Shining' and think it's one of the greatest movies ever made, doesn't mean Kubrick can do no wrong.  I still can't sit through "A Clockwork Orange"!

Will Odyssey be a flop?  Of course not.  Will it it be as big (or bigger) than Interstellar?   I HIGHLY doubt it.

replies.author: @BobSurce
replies.text: ​@wedgeantilles8575Not saying it's not possible.  Only that because Nolan made it, the opening weekend sales alone (just because his name is on it) will likely prevent it from flopping.  It's usually the second weekend sales that tell the story and decide whether it continues to make money after that.
